title: Revisiting Deepfake Detection: Chronological Continual Learning and the Limits of Generalization
authors: Not specified in the provided text
abstract: The paper addresses challenges in deepfake detection (DFD) due to continuously emerging deepfake generators. Traditional static training paradigms struggle with dynamic, real-world environments. The authors propose a chronological simulation framework for deepfake scenarios, frame the problem as a domain-incremental (DIL) task, and focus on data, training, and inference efficiency. They conducted over 600 simulations and observed that while some methods retained knowledge of past generators, none showed strong generalization to future ones.

methodology.data_selection: Datasets selected according to a Time protocol
methodology.simulation: Custom batch extraction simulating real-world data stream scenarios

contributions: Chronological simulation framework for deepfake scenarios
Strategy to simulate task sequences as random samples from datastreams
Focus on lightweight models for real-time performance
Evaluation of data, training, and inference efficiency

results.key_findings: While some methods retained knowledge of past generators, none showed strong generalization to future ones

limitations_of_prior_work: Limited number of CL methods evaluated
future_work: Code will be released upon acceptance
